own_review.issue: Usage guard checks args.length < 1 but code unconditionally executes Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length) and reads args[1]/args[2]; invoking with 1-2 args throws IllegalArgumentException/ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException stack trace instead of the usage message (guard should be < 3). Even 'FluoInit conn.props -h' crashes before help can print.

updateSharedConfig now copies EVERY property not prefixed fluo.connection.* into ZK CONFIG_SHARED - this includes fluo.accumulo.user and fluo.accumulo.password (and deprecated fluo.client.accumulo.password). Plaintext Accumulo credentials are persisted in Zookeeper shared config, downloadable by anything that can read ZK, and mergeZookeeperConfig then silently overrides locally-supplied credentials in every client/worker/oracle.

FluoClientImpl/FluoOracleImpl/FluoWorkerImpl each call FluoAdminImpl.mergeZookeeperConfig, and Environment calls it again - duplicate ZK round-trip per process; also ZK values take precedence over locally passed config, so local overrides become impossible.
Dead statement 'Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length);' whose result is discarded (the later method.invoke correctly recomputes it). Harmless but should be removed.

main() catches all exceptions, logs, and falls out of main -> JVM exits with status 0 on failure; the fluo script has already written a pid file and printed 'Started Oracle', so failures are silent. Same in FluoWorker.java.

Catch block logs and returns normally (exit 0). setup_service in the fluo script relies on this command to populate the app lib dir; on failure the worker/oracle would start without observer jars and the script would not notice.
stop sends SIGKILL (kill -s KILL) to pid files - no graceful shutdown for workers with in-flight transactions, and stale-pid reuse can kill unrelated processes.

setup_service references global $app which is only exported for oracle|worker|init commands; for 'exec' it is empty, so run/log dirs are computed as $basedir/run/ and $FLUO_LOG_DIR/ (mkdir of wrong dirs).
Copy-paste bug: declares 'File connectionProps = ...fluo-conn.properties' then asserts 'applicationProps.exists()' (already asserted earlier) instead of connectionProps.exists().
getJarsFromClasspath casts the ClassLoader to URLClassLoader - throws ClassCastException on Java 9+ (era-appropriate for Java 8 but fragile).
Comment link rewritten from apache/fluo issue to https://github.com/apache/incubator/issues/820 - wrong repository, looks like a bad search/replace.

Several unused imports (URI, FileUtils, SimpleConfiguration, FluoAdminImpl, hadoop Configuration/FileSystem/Path) and unused 'log' field; FluoGetProp has the same plus a usage-string typo ('FluoGetProps'); FluoInit -h exits with status 1 instead of 0.
ScanOptions JCommander class is duplicated verbatim between FluoScan and the (deprecated) AppRunner inner class, plus a third data-holder ScanUtil.ScanOpts - one shared definition would do.

points.note: Verified real at pr-head lines 286-287: connectionProps declared, applicationProps asserted. Right file, exact description; line number off but locatable.
False - the PR updated this exact javadoc to CONNECTION_ZOOKEEPERS_PROP (visible in the inline diff and confirmed on pr-883-head). Claims something was not updated when it was.

points.point: AppRunner.scan() return type long->void breaking change
points.note: Verified real; public abstract class, compile-time break for callers using the count.
Verified: identical JCommander blocks in FluoScan and AppRunner plus ScanUtil.ScanOpts holder.
Observation real but javadoc with @since 1.2.0 was already added; no actionable issue.

points.point: FluoAdminImpl constructor validation moved to initialize()
FluoCommand @Deprecated lacks javadoc pointing to replacement
getApplicationName() now throws NoSuchElementException instead of IllegalArgumentException
points.note: Verified: old path threw IllegalArgumentException('Application name cannot be null') via verifyApplicationName; new code throws NoSuchElementException. Real public-API exception-type change.

points.point: FluoList static oracleExists 'verify it does not require connection state'
points.note: Hedged verify-ask; static overload exists and is self-contained.

missed: FluoInit arg-crash: args.length<1 guard vs unconditional args[1]/args[2]/copyOfRange(args,3,..) - crashes with stack trace on 1-2 args (visible in truncated inline diff)
Accumulo credentials (fluo.accumulo.user/password) written into ZK shared config by new updateSharedConfig non-connection-prefix filter (the filter change WAS visible in the inline diff at its tail)
Human point: FluoExec should pass the connection config through / only set app name on the new config (FluoExec.java:66)

points.point: FluoExec 'Bug': discarded copyOfRange means invoked class receives ALL arguments
points.note: The dead statement is real, but the stated consequence is fabricated: method.invoke on the next lines passes a fresh Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length), so the target main receives only trailing args. Labeled a Bug on a false premise.
Factually true, but in the new design accumulo creds intentionally come from ZK shared config after merge; suggested fix is misguided.
URI is unused, but Map IS used (Map.Entry loop at line 58); half-false and misses the larger set of genuinely unused imports.
Verified real; script depends on it for populating app lib dir.

The described copy-paste bug is real but lives in FluoConfigurationTest.java:286-287; ScanTest.java is 107 lines long and contains no such code. Misattributed file+line despite having git tools - unusable as a review comment, but the underlying defect exists.

Fabricated failure mode: verifyNotNull only throws when the consulted value is null; if the old property is set and read successfully no error is produced, so 'error will say ... even though the code successfully reads it' cannot happen.

points.point: docs/applications.md example missing required Accumulo user/password/instance
points.note: False: under the new design FluoClientImpl only requires connection props (app name) up front, then merges Accumulo creds from ZK shared config; the trimmed example is the PR's intent and works.

points.point: Distribution config files 'cannot be verified in visible portion, confirm added'
points.note: Properly hedged, but v6 had pr_files/pr_file_diff and could simply have verified; the files are present (fluo-conn.properties, fluo-app.properties, fluo.properties.deprecated all added).

missed: FluoInit arg-crash (args.length<1 guard) - visible in inline diff and via git tools
Accumulo credentials written into ZK shared config by updateSharedConfig prefix filter
Human point: FluoExec config pass-through design (v6 commented on FluoExec but about a different, partly fabricated issue)

qualitative: Both candidates missed the two highest-value defects - the FluoInit argument-count crash and Accumulo credentials being persisted into Zookeeper shared config - even though both were visible in the truncated inline diff, and both also missed the human's FluoExec config pass-through point. v5 is the cleaner review: four solid verified findings (the FluoConfigurationTest assertion bug, the scan() return-type break, constructor-validation removal, and the exception-type change) against one absence-fabrication (claiming the FluoAdmin javadoc still referenced the old property when the PR updated it). v6, despite having full per-file git access, fabricated three findings (a false 'all args passed to invoked main' bug in FluoExec, an impossible getAccumuloPassword error scenario, and a wrong claim that the docs example is missing required credentials) and misattributed the real test-assertion bug to ScanTest.java:195, a location that does not exist. v6's extra tooling produced no accuracy gain - its strongest unique point (FluoGetJars silent failure) is outweighed by hallucinated bug mechanics, so v5 wins on precision.
